devanagari: श्रीमद्भागवत महापुराण । अष्टमः स्कन्धःषोडशोऽध्यायः । कश्यपस्य देवमात्रेऽदित्यै पयोव्रतोपदेशःश्रीशुक उवाचएवं पुत्रेषु नष्टेषु देवमातादिति...
english_devnagari: śrīmadbhāgavata mahāpurāṇa aṣṭamaḥ skandhaḥṣoḍaśo kaśyapasya devamātre hṛte triviṣṭape daityaiḥ paryatapyad anāthavat

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
When her sons thus disappeared and the celestial regions were arrogated to themselves by Daityas (Diti’s son), Aditi, the mother of gods, was tormented like a helpless person.

devanagari: एकदा कश्यपस्तस्या आश्रमं भगवान् अगात् । निरुत्सवं निरानन्दं समाधेर्विरतश्चिरात् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: ekadā kaśyapastasyā āśramaṃ bhagavān agāt nirutsavaṃ nirānandaṃ samādhervirataścirāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: Once, rising from his spiritual meditation of a long duration, the worshipful sage Kāśyapa (Aditi’s husband) visited her hermitage which was cheerless and gloomy (devoid of joyful atmosphere).

devanagari: स पत्‍नीं दीनवदनां कृतासनपरिग्रहः । सभाजितो यथान्यायं इदमाह कुरूद्वह ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa pat‍nīṃ dīnavadanāṃ kṛtāsanaparigrahaḥ sabhājito yathānyāyaṃ idamāha kurūdvaha
translation: Oh best of Kurus! Received (by his wife) with due formalities and occupying the seat (offered by her), he spoke thus to his wife, whose countenance was mournful.

devanagari: अप्यभद्रं न विप्राणां भद्रे लोकेऽधुनाऽगतम् । न धर्मस्य न लोकस्य मृत्योश्छन्दानुवर्तिनः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: apyabhadraṃ na viprāṇāṃ bhadre loke na dharmasya na lokasya mṛtyośchandānuvartinaḥ
translation: Oh auspicious lady! I trust nothing inauspicious has befallen[1] the Brāhmaṇas in the world, of late. I believe nothing calamitous has happened to Dharma. I hope that misfortune has not overtaken the people who are subject to the whims of death.[2]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
For āgatam, Bhāgavata Candrikā reads gavām: ‘in the case of the cows as well’.

Footnote [2]:
Padaratnāvalī takes chandānuvartinaḥ as an adj. qualifying mṛtyu implying ‘through the god of death who acts according to whim’.

devanagari: अपि वाकुशलं किञ्चिद्‍गृहेषु गृहमेधिनि । धर्मस्यार्थस्य कामस्य यत्र योगो ह्ययोगिनाम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: api vākuśalaṃ kiñcid‍gṛheṣu gṛhamedhini dharmasyārthasya kāmasya yatra yogo hyayoginām
translation: Or was there any obstruction in carrying out righteous duties with respect to Dharma, Artha and Kāma in your household where people who do not follow the practice of yoga, get the fruits thereof (by disinterested performance of prescribed house-hold duties), Oh mistress of the household.

devanagari: अपि वा अतिथयोऽभ्येत्य कुटुंबासक्तया त्वया । गृहादपूजिता याताः प्रत्युत्थानेन वा क्वचित् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: api vā atithayo gṛhādapūjitā yātāḥ pratyutthānena vā kvacit

translation: I believe guests, having visited your house, have not gone back, at any time, without being duly received with courtesies like going forth to welcome them, as you might have been engaged in household duties.

devanagari: गृहेषु येष्वतिथयो नार्चिताः सलिलैरपि । यदि निर्यान्ति ते नूनं फेरुराजगृहोपमाः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛheṣu yeṣvatithayo nārcitāḥ salilairapi yadi niryānti te nūnaṃ pherurājagṛhopamāḥ

translation: If new-comers visiting a house go away from it without being honoured with the offer of water at least, such houses are certainly no better than the dens of big jackals.

devanagari: अप्यग्नयस्तु वेलायां न हुता हविषा सति । त्वयोद्विग्नधिया भद्रे प्रोषिते मयि कर्हिचित् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: apyagnayastu velāyāṃ na hutā haviṣā sati tvayodvignadhiyā bhadre proṣite mayi karhicit

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ hu class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ hu class 3 verb]

translation: Have not the sacred fires been offered oblations at the proper time on any day by you, as your mind must have been stricken with anxiety due to my departure, Oh fair lady!

devanagari: यत्पूजया कामदुघान् याति लोकान् गृहान्वितः । ब्राह्मणोऽग्निश्च वै विष्णोः सर्वदेवात्मनो मुखम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: yatpūjayā kāmadughān yāti lokān gṛhānvitaḥ brāhmaṇo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: The Brāhmaṇa and the (sacrificial) fire by worshipping whom a householder attains to regions (like svarga) yielding all the desired objects, are verily the mouth of Lord Viṣṇu, the soul of all gods.

devanagari: अपि सर्वे कुशलिनः तव पुत्रा मनस्विनि । लक्षयेऽस्वस्थमात्मानं भवत्या लक्षणैरहम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: api sarve kuśalinaḥ tava putrā manasvini lakṣaye
translation: Oh high-minded lady! Are all your sons doing well and happy? I perceive through your outward expressions (like pale-facedness) that your heart is not at ease.

devanagari: अदितिरुवाचभद्रं द्विजगवां ब्रह्मन् धर्मस्यास्य जनस्य च । त्रिवर्गस्य परं क्षेत्रं गृहमेधिन् गृहा इमे ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: aditiruvācabhadraṃ dvijagavāṃ brahman dharmasyāsya janasya ca trivargasya paraṃ kṣetraṃ gṛhamedhin gṛhā ime

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Aditi replied:
Oh Brahman! It is all well with the twice-born persons (like Brāhmaṇas), cows; so is the case with righteousness and with this person (me). Oh Lord of the house! This house (of yours) is the most excellent source of the three objectives of human life (viz. dharma, artha and kāma).

devanagari: अग्नयोऽतिथयो भृत्या भिक्षवो ये च लिप्सवः । सर्वं भगवतो ब्रह्मन् अनुध्यानान्न रिष्यति ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: agnayo sarvaṃ bhagavato brahman anudhyānānna riṣyati
translation: It is due to my devoted contemplation about your worshipful self that nothing is lacking and the sacrificial fires, guests, servants and mendicants desirous of getting something, are properly attended to.

devanagari: को नु मे भगवन् कामो न सम्पद्येत मानसः । यस्या भवान् प्रजाध्यक्ष एवं धर्मान् प्रभाषते ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: ko nu me bhagavan kāmo na sampadyeta mānasaḥ yasyā bhavān prajādhyakṣa evaṃ dharmān prabhāṣate
translation: Oh worshipful lord! What desire cherished in my heart can remain unfulfilled when you, the lord of creation and the guardian of the well-being of my children in this way, advise me on religious matters.

devanagari: तवैव मारीच मनःशरीरजाः । प्रजा इमाः सत्त्वरजस्तमोजुषः । समो भवान् तास्वसुरादिषु प्रभो । तथापि भक्तं भजते महेश्वरः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tavaiva mārīca manaḥśarīrajāḥ prajā imāḥ sattvarajastamojuṣaḥ samo bhavān tāsvasurādiṣu prabho tathāpi bhaktaṃ bhajate maheśvaraḥ
translation: Oh son of Marīci! All these beings imbrued predominantly with sattva, rajas and tamas severally are born of your mind or body. (It is quite natural that) you are therefore equally attached to them all. But even the Supreme Lord (who creates the whole of the universe has special attachment to his devotees.

devanagari: तस्मादीश भजन्त्या मे श्रेयश्चिन्तय सुव्रत । हृतश्रियो हृतस्थानान् सपत्‍नैः पाहि नः प्रभो ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmādīśa bhajantyā me śreyaścintaya suvrata hṛtaśriyo hṛtasthānān sapat‍naiḥ pāhi naḥ prabho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śrā class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ śrā class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ śrā class 4 verb]
[nominative single from √ śrai class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ śrai class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ śrai class 4 verb]

translation: Oh Lord! Be pleased to think of the well being of mine who have been serving you, Oh excellent observer of vows! We (I and my sons) have been deprived of our wealth and have been ousted from our abodes by the sons of my rival (Diti). Kindly protect us, Oh Lord!

devanagari: परैर्विवासिता साहं मग्ना व्यसनसागरे । ऐश्वर्यं श्रीर्यशः स्थानं हृतानि प्रबलैर्मम ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: parairvivāsitā sāhaṃ magnā vyasanasāgare aiśvaryaṃ śrīryaśaḥ sthānaṃ hṛtāni prabalairmama

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ majj class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ majj class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ majj class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ majj class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ majj class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb]

translation: Being deprived of my power, splendour, glory and place of residence by powerful enemies, I have been exiled and have consequently immersed in a sea of calamities.

devanagari: यथा तानि पुनः साधो प्रपद्येरन् ममात्मजाः । तथा विधेहि कल्याणं धिया कल्याणकृत्तम ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā tāni punaḥ sādho prapadyeran mamātmajāḥ tathā vidhehi kalyāṇaṃ dhiyā kalyāṇakṛttama

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: Oh foremost guardian of our welfare! Be pleased to think out such effective device by your intellect as will enable my sons to recover them all.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाचएवं अभ्यर्थितोऽदित्या कस्तामाह स्मयन्निव । अहो मायाबलं विष्णोः स्नेहबद्धं इदं जगत् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaevaṃ abhyarthito aho māyābalaṃ viṣṇoḥ snehabaddhaṃ idaṃ jagat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Kāśyapa who was thus entreated by Aditi, spoke to her as if with a bit of smile: “Oh how mighty is the power of Lord Viṣṇu’s delusive potency (māyā)! The whole world is bound down with affection.

devanagari: क्व देहो भौतिकोऽनात्मा क्व चात्मा प्रकृतेः परः । कस्य के पतिपुत्राद्या मोह एव हि कारणम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: kva deho bhautiko kasya ke patiputrādyā moha eva hi kāraṇam
translation: There is absolutely no relation between the material body which is different from the soul and the soul who transcends Prakṛti. Whose and who are the husband, sons and other relatives? Delusion alone is the cause of this attachment.

devanagari: उपतिष्ठस्व पुरुषं भगवन्तं जनार्दनम् । सर्वभूतगुहावासं वासुदेवं जगद्‍गुरुम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: upatiṣṭhasva puruṣaṃ bhagavantaṃ janārdanam sarvabhūtaguhāvāsaṃ vāsudevaṃ jagad‍gurum
translation: Worship therefore the Supreme Person, the glorious Lord Janārdana, the all-pervading God who resides in the heart of all beings, and is the Father and Preceptor of the Universe.

devanagari: स विधास्यति ते कामान् हरिर्दीनानुकंपनः । अमोघा भगवद्‍भक्तिः न इतरेति मतिर्मम ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vidhāsyati te kāmān harirdīnānukaṃpanaḥ amoghā bhagavad‍bhaktiḥ na itareti matirmama
translation: Lord Hari is merciful unto the afflicted and shall grant all your desires (even if there may be some deficiencies in devotion). Unfailingly fruitful is the devotion unto the Lord (and not to any other god). This is my conviction (about the efficacy of devotion).

devanagari: अदितिरुवाचकेनाहं विधिना ब्रह्मन् उपस्थास्ये जगत्पतिम् । यथा मे सत्यसंकल्पो विदध्यात् स मनोरथम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: aditiruvācakenāhaṃ vidhinā brahman upasthāsye jagatpatim yathā me satyasaṃkalpo vidadhyāt sa manoratham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Aditi requested:
By what procedure I should worship the Protector of the world so that he, of effective mental resolve, may grant me my desired object, Oh Brahman!

devanagari: आदिश त्वं द्विजश्रेष्ठ विधिं तदुपधावनम् । आशु तुष्यति मे देवः सीदन्त्याः सह पुत्रकैः । कश्यप उवाचएतन्मे भगवान् पृष्टः प्रजाकामस्य पद्मज...
english_devnagari: ādiśa tvaṃ dvijaśreṣṭha vidhiṃ tadupadhāvanam āśu tuṣyati me devaḥ sīdantyāḥ saha putrakaiḥ kaśyapa uvācaetanme bhagavān pṛṣṭaḥ prajākāmasya padmajaḥ yadāha te pravakṣyāmi vrataṃ keśavatoṣaṇam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [ablative single from √ sad class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ sad class 1 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Kaśyapa said:
The worshipful Lotus-born god Brahmā was accosted for this by me when desirous of progeny. As narrated by him, I shall now explain to you the sacred vow that leads to the propitiation of Lord Viṣṇu (Keśava.)

devanagari: फाल्गुनस्यामले पक्षे द्वादशाहं पयोव्रतम् । अर्चयेत् अरविन्दाक्षं भक्त्या परमयान्वितः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: phālgunasyāmale pakṣe dvādaśāhaṃ payovratam arcayet aravindākṣaṃ bhaktyā paramayānvitaḥ
translation: Subsisting purely on milk during the first twelve days of the bright half of the month of Phālguna, one should worship the Lotus-eyed Lord Viṣṇu with the utmost devotion.

devanagari: सिनीवाल्यां मृदालिप्य स्नायात् क्रोडविदीर्णया । यदि लभ्येत वै स्रोतसि एतं मंत्रं उदीरयेत् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: sinīvālyāṃ mṛdālipya snāyāt kroḍavidīrṇayā yadi labhyeta vai srotasi etaṃ maṃtraṃ udīrayet

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: On the day preceding the new moon (when the moon rises with a scarcely visible crescent) one should besmear one’s body with earth, turned over by the wild-boar, if available and take bath. While standing in the middle of the current, he should recite the following mantra:

devanagari: त्वं देव्यादिवराहेण रसायाः स्थानमिच्छता । उद्धृतासि नमस्तुभ्यं पाप्मानं मे प्रणाशय ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṃ devyādivarāheṇa rasāyāḥ sthānamicchatā uddhṛtāsi namastubhyaṃ pāpmānaṃ me praṇāśaya
translation: “Oh goddess! You were lifted up from Rasātala (the nether-most region) by the Lord (assuming the form of) the Primitive Boar, with a view to secure a place (for living beings). I respectfully bow to you. Be pleased to absolve me of all my sins”.

devanagari: निर्वर्तितात्मनियमो देवं अर्चेत् समाहितः । अर्चायां स्थण्डिले सूर्ये जले वह्नौ गुरौ अपि ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: nirvartitātmaniyamo devaṃ arcet samāhitaḥ arcāyāṃ sthaṇḍile sūrye jale vahnau gurau api

translation: After completing one’s daily routine of religious duties, with a serene and concentrated mind, one should worship God either in an image, or on a consecrated altar or in the sun, water, fire or even in the preceptor. (For invoking the diety [deity?], the following nine Mantras are to be recited).

devanagari: नमस्तुभ्यं भगवते पुरुषाय महीयसे । सर्वभूतनिवासाय वासुदेवाय साक्षिणे ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: namastubhyaṃ bhagavate puruṣāya mahīyase sarvabhūtanivāsāya vāsudevāya sākṣiṇe
translation: Obeisance to you the glorious Lord, the Supreme Person, the greatest of the great, the abode of all created beings, (and hence called) the all-pervading Vāsudeva the witness (to all and everything).

devanagari: नमोऽव्यक्ताय सूक्ष्माय प्रधानपुरुषाय च । चतुर्विंशद्‍गुणज्ञाय गुणसंख्यानहेतवे ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: namo caturviṃśad‍guṇajñāya guṇasaṃkhyānahetave
translation: Salutations to the unmanifest[3] and the subtle, who is both Prakṛti (Primordial matter) and Puruṣa (the spirit)—pervading them as their antar-yāmin (the indwelling Principle), who comprehends twenty-four Principles[4] and is the founder of the Sāṃkhya system.[5]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Cf. Muṇḍaka up. 1.1.16.

Footnote [4]:
These principles are subsidiary causes and as his attributes they do not exist separately. Sārārthadarśinī thinks that the 24 guṇas are the categories of Vaiśeṣikas and may mean Sāṅkhyatattvas also.

Footnote [5]:
Padaratnāvalī reads: guṇasaṃsthāna-hetave.

devanagari: नमो द्विशीर्ष्णे त्रिपदे चतुःश्रृंगाय तन्तवे । सप्तहस्ताय यज्ञाय त्रयीविद्यात्मने नमः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: namo dviśīrṣṇe tripade catuḥśrṛṃgāya tantave saptahastāya yajñāya trayīvidyātmane namaḥ
translation: [6] Hail to you who have two heads, three feet, four horns; who confer fruit of sacrifices etc., the possessor of seven hands, who are himself the sacrifice and the soul of the three Vedas (or are described in three Vedas).
purport:
Footnote [6]:
(i). Bhāvāratha Dīpikā compares this verse with the famous description of the Fire- god Catvāri Śṛṅgā trayo asya pādā etc. in the Ṛg Veda Saṃhitā 4.58.3 and quotes Yāska’s explanation as follows:
Two heads—the rites called prāyaṇīya & Udayanīya.
The three feet—three savanas or pressing out of the soma juice which is to be done in the morning, at midday and in the evening.
Four horns—the four Vedas.
Seven arms—the seven Vedic metres (chandas) viz. Gāyatrī, Triṣṭubh, Anuṣṭubh. Bṛhatī. Paṅkti, Jagatī and Uṣṇik.
(ii) Padaratnāvalī has accepted the above explanation of the metaphorical description but the 2nd line: ‘Salutation to Lord Viṣṇu who is the antaryāmin (ātmane) of the performers of Soma sacrifice as enjoined in the Vedas (Trayī Vidyā).
(iii) Bhāgavata Candrikā gives a totally different interpretation.
The whole universe of name and form is an attribute of the Lord. He is metaphorically described as a bull (Vṛṣabha) with four horns viz., names (nouns), verbs, indeclinables and prefixes (nāma-ākhyāta-nipāta-upasargāḥ). The three feet are three divisions of time: past, present and future; the two heads are: the essential attribute (pravṛtti-nimitta) and that in which the attribute lies (tadāśrayaḥ). The seven hands are the seven cases of declension (of nouns etc.). The Lord is known through Vedas. Hence he is the soul of the Vedas. He is Viṣṇu or sacrifice (yajño vai Viṣṇuḥ) through whom men can extend the performance of sacrifice (tantave).

devanagari: नमः शिवाय रुद्राय नमः शक्तिधराय च । सर्वविद्याधिपतये भूतानां पतये नमः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: namaḥ śivāya rudrāya namaḥ śaktidharāya ca sarvavidyādhipataye bhūtānāṃ pataye namaḥ
translation: [7] I bow to you who are Śiva (all auspiciousness) and Rudra. Hail to you the (wielder) of Śakti (Power). Salutation to you the master of all lores[8] or knowledge and the Protector of all created beings.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā regards Viṣṇu as the antaryāmin of Rudra (and other gods) as he is the real creator, protector and destroyer of the world.

Footnote [8]:
Sarva-vidyādhipataye—The preceptor of the knowledge which helps to achieve the four puruṣārthas—Bālaprabodhini

devanagari: नमो हिरण्यगर्भाय प्राणाय जगदात्मने । योगैश्वर्यशरीराय नमस्ते योगहेतवे ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: namo hiraṇyagarbhāya prāṇāya jagadātmane yogaiśvaryaśarīrāya namaste yogahetave
translation: Obeisance to Hiraṇyagarbha (god Brahmā), Prāṇa (i.e. sūtrātman, the soul of the subtle cosmic body), the soul of the universe. Hail to you, the embodiment of the highest powers acquired by yoga and the source of yoga (yogic lore).

devanagari: नमस्ते आदिदेवाय साक्षिभूताय ते नमः । नारायणाय ऋषये नराय हरये नमः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: namaste ādidevāya sākṣibhūtāya te namaḥ nārāyaṇāya ṛṣaye narāya haraye namaḥ
translation: Salutations to you the First (Eternal) God. I bow to you who are the witness (to everything). Hail to you the omniscient sage Nārāyaṇa and to Nara who are the manifestations of Hari.

devanagari: नमो मरकतश्याम वपुषेऽधिगतश्रिये । केशवाय नमस्तुभ्यं नमस्ते पीतवाससे ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: namo marakataśyāma vapuṣe keśavāya namastubhyaṃ namaste pītavāsase
translation: Bow to you whose person is dark like emerald in complexion and who have been the master of the goddess Śrī. Salutation to you, Oh Keśava, the wearer of yellow garment.

devanagari: त्वं सर्ववरदः पुंसां वरेण्य वरदर्षभ । अतस्ते श्रेयसे धीराः पादरेणुं उपासते ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṃ sarvavaradaḥ puṃsāṃ vareṇya varadarṣabha ataste śreyase dhīrāḥ pādareṇuṃ upāsate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[vocative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation: You are the conferer of all boons to people. Hence you are most sought for, Oh most prominent bestower of blessings. It is hence that wise men adore the dust of your feet for the final beatitude.

devanagari: अन्ववर्तन्त यं देवाः श्रीश्च तत्पादपद्मयोः । स्पृहयन्त इवामोदं भगवान् मे प्रसीदताम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: anvavartanta yaṃ devāḥ śrīśca tatpādapadmayoḥ spṛhayanta ivāmodaṃ bhagavān me prasīdatām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ spṛh class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ spṛh class 10 verb]

translation: May that glorious Lord be gracious to me—the Lord who is worshipped by gods and Lakṣmī, as if they desired to have the fragrance of His Lotus-like feet.

devanagari: एतैः मंत्रैः हृर्हृषीकेशं आवाहनपुरस्कृतम् । अर्चयेत् श्रद्धया युक्तः पाद्योपस्पर्शनादिभिः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: etaiḥ maṃtraiḥ hṛrhṛṣīkeśaṃ āvāhanapuraskṛtam arcayet śraddhayā yuktaḥ pādyopasparśanādibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ pad class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ pad ]
[vocative single from √ pad class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ pad ]
[nominative single from √ pad class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ pad ]
[vocative single from √ pad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pad ]
[vocative single from √ pad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pad ]
[nominative single from √ pad class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ pad ]

translation: Having reverentially established the presence of Viṣṇu, the Lord of Senses, before one’s self, by invoking him with these mantras, one should with faith, worship him, by offering water for washing the feet (pādya), for ācamana (sipping and rinsing the mouth) and other formalities of reception.

devanagari: अर्चित्वा गन्धमाल्याद्यैः पयसा स्नपयेद् विभुम् । वस्त्रोपवीताभरण पाद्योपस्पर्शनैस्ततः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: arcitvā gandhamālyādyaiḥ payasā snapayed vibhum vastropavītābharaṇa pādyopasparśanaistataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ pad class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ pad ]
[vocative single from √ pad class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ pad ]
[nominative single from √ pad class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ pad ]
[vocative single from √ pad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pad ]
[vocative single from √ pad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pad ]
[nominative single from √ pad class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ pad ]

translation: One should worship the Lord with fragrant sandal paste, flowers and other articles of worship and should perform his sacred ablution (abhiṣeka) with milk. He should then adore him by beautifying his person with clothes, sacred thread, ornaments and with water for again washing his feet and sipping water as ācamana, burning fragrant incense etc. before him, reciting all the while the twelve-syllabled mantra: OM NAMO BHAGAVATE VĀSUDEVĀYA.

devanagari: श्रृतं पयसि नैवेद्यं शाल्यन्नं विभवे सति । ससर्पिः सगुडं दत्त्वा जुहुयान् मूलविद्यया ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: śrṛtaṃ payasi naivedyaṃ śālyannaṃ vibhave sati sasarpiḥ saguḍaṃ dattvā juhuyān mūlavidyayā
translation: If one can afford it (lit. If there is sufficient wealth), one should offer fragrant, white rice cooked in milk, mixed with ghee and raw sugar, as an offering (naivedya) to him, and should pour it as oblations into the sacred fire chanting the root mantra (viz. the above-mentioned mantra of twelve syllables)[9].
purport:
Footnote [9]:
With due respect to all these commentators, the translator feels that due to the obvious influence of the Pañcarātra on these verses, the mūla-mantra must be OṂ NAMO NĀRĀYAṆĀYA and NOT Oṃ Name Bhagavate Vāsudevāya, though consistency in Mantras is essential.

devanagari: निवेदितं तद्‍भक्ताय दद्याद्‍भुञ्जीत वा स्वयम् । दत्त्वाऽचमनमर्चित्वा तांबूलं च निवेदयेत् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: niveditaṃ tad‍bhaktāya dadyād‍bhuñjīta vā svayam dattvā
translation: One may give this offering (of boiled rice) to God to a devotee (of the Lord) or should eat it oneself. Having offered water as ācamana one should offer betel-leaves (with all the components like areca-nut powder, cardamom seeds, cloves etc.) finally.

devanagari: जपेत् अष्टोत्तरशतं स्तुवीत स्तुतिभिः प्रभुम् । कृत्वा प्रदक्षिणं भूमौ प्रणमेद् दण्डवन्मुदा ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: japet aṣṭottaraśataṃ stuvīta stutibhiḥ prabhum kṛtvā pradakṣiṇaṃ bhūmau praṇamed daṇḍavanmudā
translation: One should chant this mantra (within one’s mind) one hundred and eight times, and praise God with panygerics. Circumambulating him by the right side, one should prostrate one’s self before him like a rod, with delight.

devanagari: कृत्वा शिरसि तच्छेषां देवं उद्वासयेत् ततः । द्व्यवरान् भोजयेद् विप्रान् पायसेन यथोचितम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛtvā śirasi taccheṣāṃ devaṃ udvāsayet tataḥ dvyavarān bhojayed viprān pāyasena yathocitam
translation: Reverentially placing on one’s own head the remnants (flowers etc.) of the worship, one should pray the Deity to retire. One should then feed at least a couple of Brāhmaṇas with rice cooked in milk (and mixed with gur etc.).

devanagari: भुञ्जीत तैरनुज्ञातः सेष्टः शेषं सभाजितैः । ब्रह्मचार्यथ तद् रात्र्यां श्वो भूते प्रथमेऽहनि ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: bhuñjīta tairanujñātaḥ seṣṭaḥ śeṣaṃ sabhājitaiḥ brahmacāryatha tad rātryāṃ śvo bhūte prathame

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jñā class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ jñā class 9 verb]

translation: Obtaining the assent of the Brāhmaṇas who are duly honoured (by giving betel-leaves, gift of money etc.), one should partake of the remaining food in company of his dear ones. On the night of the first day, one should observe strict continence. And on the next morning, he should take bath. Keeping himself unḍefiled according to the procedure laid down, and with composed and concentrated mind, he should perform the ablution of the deity with milk, and worship him (and as per details of worship given above) and thus observe the vow every day), till the period of the observance is completed.

devanagari: स्नातः शुचिर्यथोक्तेन विधिना सुसमाहितः । पयसा स्नापयित्वार्चेद् यावद् व्रतसमापनम् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: snātaḥ śuciryathoktena vidhinā susamāhitaḥ payasā snāpayitvārced yāvad vratasamāpanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ snā class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ snā class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ snā class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ snā class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ snā class 4 verb]
[ablative single from √ snā class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ snā class 2 verb]

translation: Obtaining the assent of the Brāhmaṇas who are duly honoured (by giving betel-leaves, gift of money etc.), one should partake of the remaining food in company of his dear ones. On the night of the first day, one should observe strict continence. And on the next morning, he should take bath. Keeping himself unḍefiled according to the procedure laid down, and with composed and concentrated mind, he should perform the ablution of the deity with milk, and worship him (and as per details of worship given above) and thus observe the vow every day), till the period of the observance is completed.

devanagari: पयोभक्षो व्रतमिदं चरेत् विष्णु अर्चनादृतः । पूर्ववत् जुहुयादग्निं ब्राह्मणांश्चापि भोजयेत् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: payobhakṣo vratamidaṃ caret viṣṇu arcanādṛtaḥ pūrvavat juhuyādagniṃ brāhmaṇāṃścāpi bhojayet
translation: Subsisting on milk-food and with full faith and devotion in the worship of Viṣṇu, one should continue to observe this vow. He should offer oblations to the sacred fire as before, and give food to the Brāhmaṇas as well.

devanagari: एवं तु अहः अहः कुर्याद् द्वादशाहं पयोव्रतम् । हरेः आराधनं होमं अर्हणं द्विजतर्पणम् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ tu ahaḥ ahaḥ kuryād dvādaśāhaṃ payovratam hareḥ ārādhanaṃ homaṃ arhaṇaṃ dvijatarpaṇam
translation: In this way, observing the vow of ‘subsistence on milk’ from day to day, he should, for a period of twelve days, perform the propitiation of Lord Hari, offering oblations to the sacred fire, worship of the image and gratification of Brāhmaṇas (with food, gift of money etc.).

devanagari: प्रतिपत्दिनं आरभ्य यावत् शुक्लत्रयोदशीम् । ब्रह्मचर्यमधःस्वप्नं स्नानं त्रिषवणं चरेत् ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: pratipatdinaṃ ārabhya yāvat śuklatrayodaśīm brahmacaryamadhaḥsvapnaṃ snānaṃ triṣavaṇaṃ caret
translation: Beginning from the first day (of the bright half of the month of Phālguna) to the thirteenth, he should observe strict continence, sleep on bare ground and take bath three times a day (viz. in the morning, at midday and in the evening).

devanagari: वर्जयेत् असद् आलापं भोगान् उच्चावचान् तथा । अहिंस्रः सर्वभूतानां वासुदेवपरायणः ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: varjayet asad ālāpaṃ bhogān uccāvacān tathā ahiṃsraḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ vāsudevaparāyaṇaḥ
translation: He should avoid speaking the untruth and on unholy topics (as well as with evil persons). He should refrain from pleasures of high or low order; he should abstain from injury to any being, and be absolutely devoted to Vāsudeva only.

devanagari: त्रयोदश्यां अथो विष्णोः स्नपनं पञ्चकैर्विभोः । कारयेत् शास्त्रदृष्टेन विधिना विधिकोविदैः ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: trayodaśyāṃ atho viṣṇoḥ snapanaṃ pañcakairvibhoḥ kārayet śāstradṛṣṭena vidhinā vidhikovidaiḥ
translation: On the thirtieth day, he should arrange for the ablution of Lord Viṣṇu with five substances (viz. milk, curds, ghee, honey and sugar which are collectively known as pañcāmṛta) according to the procedure laid down in the Śāstras, and under the guidance of the experts in them.

devanagari: पूजां च महतीं कुर्यात् वित्त शाठ्य विवर्जितः । चरुं निरूप्य पयसि शिपिविष्टाय विष्णवे ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: pūjāṃ ca mahatīṃ kuryāt vitta śāṭhya vivarjitaḥ caruṃ nirūpya payasi śipiviṣṭāya viṣṇave
translation: Without any mean miserliness in expending money, he should perform the worship of Viṣṇu on a grand scale. For the sake of Viṣṇu who pervaded all light, he should cook rice in milk (mixed with ghee etc.) and with fully concentrated and serene mind he should offer oblations to the sacred fire, and through it propitiate the Supreme Man. He should also offer food of excellent quality (containing articles of six types of tastes and free from any impurity or nonvegetarian food) which may be enjoyable and gratifying to the Supreme Person.

devanagari: श्रृतेन तेन पुरुषं यजेत सुसमाहितः । नैवेद्यं चातिगुणवद् दद्यात् पुरुषतुष्टिदम् ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrṛtena tena puruṣaṃ yajeta susamāhitaḥ naivedyaṃ cātiguṇavad dadyāt puruṣatuṣṭidam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]

translation: Without any mean miserliness in expending money, he should perform the worship of Viṣṇu on a grand scale. For the sake of Viṣṇu who pervaded all light, he should cook rice in milk (mixed with ghee etc.) and with fully concentrated and serene mind he should offer oblations to the sacred fire, and through it propitiate the Supreme Man. He should also offer food of excellent quality (containing articles of six types of tastes and free from any impurity or nonvegetarian food) which may be enjoyable and gratifying to the Supreme Person.

devanagari: आचार्यं ज्ञानसम्पन्नं वस्त्राभरणधेनुभिः । तोषयेत् ऋत्विजश्चैव तद् विद्धि आराधनं हरेः ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: ācāryaṃ jñānasampannaṃ vastrābharaṇadhenubhiḥ toṣayet ṛtvijaścaiva tad viddhi ārādhanaṃ hareḥ
translation: He should then gratify the learned preceptor (who conducted the worship) as well as those who carried out the sacrificial part, with articles of dress, ornaments and cows. He should note that to be also the propitiation of Lord Hari.

devanagari: भोजयेत् तान्गुणवता सदन्नेन शुचिस्मिते । अन्यांश्च ब्राह्मणान् शक्त्या ये च तत्र समागताः ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: bhojayet tānguṇavatā sadannena śucismite anyāṃśca brāhmaṇān śaktyā ye ca tatra samāgatāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sad class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ sad class 6 verb]

translation: Oh lady with charming smiles! One should then cater to them dainty food of high quality as also to other Brāhmaṇas who might have come there, according to one’s (financial) capacity.

devanagari: दक्षिणां गुरवे दद्याद् ऋत्विग्भ्यश्च यथार्हतः । अन्नाद्येन अश्वपाकांश्च प्रीणयेत् समुपागतान् ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: dakṣiṇāṃ gurave dadyād ṛtvigbhyaśca yathārhataḥ annādyena aśvapākāṃśca prīṇayet samupāgatān
translation: One should then give dakṣiṇā (gift for performing religious rites) according to merits, to the preceptor as well as to other Brāhmaṇas. One should also gratify with food etc. all those who have assembled there, including caṇḍālas.

devanagari: भुक्तवत्सु च सर्वेषु दीनान्ध कृपणादिषु । विष्णोस्तत् प्रीणनं विद्वान् भुञ्जीत सह बन्धुभिः ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: bhuktavatsu ca sarveṣu dīnāndha kṛpaṇādiṣu viṣṇostat prīṇanaṃ vidvān bhuñjīta saha bandhubhiḥ
translation: Having gratified all indigent, blind, pitiable persons with food and understanding that that gratification is the real propitiation of Viṣṇu, one should lastly enjoy the food along with one’s relatives.

devanagari: नृत्यवादित्रगीतैश्च स्तुतिभिः स्वस्तिवाचकैः । कारयेत् तत्कथाभिश्च पूजां भगवतोऽन्वहम् ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: nṛtyavāditragītaiśca stutibhiḥ svastivācakaiḥ kārayet tatkathābhiśca pūjāṃ bhagavato
translation: Every day the worship of the Lord should be conducted with dancing, instrumental and vocal music, recitation of glorifying hymns and benedictory sayings and narration of the holy episodes of the Lord.

devanagari: एतत् पयोव्रतं नाम पुरुषाराधनं परम् । पितामहेनाभिहितं मया ते समुदाहृतम् ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: etat payovrataṃ nāma puruṣārādhanaṃ param pitāmahenābhihitaṃ mayā te samudāhṛtam
translation: This is called Payovrata, the most excellent vow for propitiating the Supreme Person. What has been explained to me by god Brahmā has been communicated to you.

devanagari: त्वं चानेन महाभागे सम्यक् चीर्णेन केशवम् । आत्मना शुद्धभावेन नियतात्मा भजाव्ययम् ॥ ५९ ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṃ cānena mahābhāge samyak cīrṇena keśavam ātmanā śuddhabhāvena niyatātmā bhajāvyayam
translation: Oh most fortunate lady! By properly and correctly observing this vow, and with your heart full of purity and devotion, and mind under control, worship Lord Keśava.

devanagari: अयं वै सर्वयज्ञाख्यः सर्वव्रतमिति स्मृतम् । तपःसारं इदं भद्रे दानं च ईश्वरतर्पणम् ॥ ६० ॥
english_devnagari: ayaṃ vai sarvayajñākhyaḥ sarvavratamiti smṛtam tapaḥsāraṃ idaṃ bhadre dānaṃ ca īśvaratarpaṇam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: This is traditionally regarded as the perfect (or the essence of all kinds of) sacrifice and the perfect vow (the essence of all vows). This is the essence of penance and donations which leads to the gratification of the Lord, Oh fair lady!

devanagari: ते एव नियमाः साक्षात् ते एव च यमोत्तमाः । तपो दानं व्रतं यज्ञो येन तुष्यति अधोक्षजः ॥ ६१ ॥
english_devnagari: te eva niyamāḥ sākṣāt te eva ca yamottamāḥ tapo dānaṃ vrataṃ yajño yena tuṣyati adhokṣajaḥ
translation: That by which god Viṣṇu, the Supra-Sensuous deity, is propitiated are the real Niyamas (restraints of the mind), and the excellent yamas (controls of bodily discipline), the real penance, gifts, vow and sacrifice.

devanagari: तस्मात् एतद्व्रतं भद्रे प्रयता श्रद्धयाचर । भगवान् परितुष्टस्ते वरानाशु विधास्यति ॥ ६२ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmāt etadvrataṃ bhadre prayatā śraddhayācara bhagavān parituṣṭaste varānāśu vidhāsyati
translation:
Therefore, Oh fair lady, with utmost self-discipline and devotion, you observe this vow, being pleased wherewith the Lord shall soon confer your cherished boons on you.
[back to top]

devanagari: गन्धधूपादिभिश्चार्चेद् द्वादशाक्षरविद्यया ॥ ३९५ ॥
english_devnagari: gandhadhūpādibhiścārced dvādaśākṣaravidyayā
